title: Junior Automation Project Engineer
description:
About No deviation

At No deviation, we are dedicated to delivering patient-centric solutions that revolutionize the pharmaceutical landscape. As a dynamic expert in engineering consultancy, Commissioning Qualification Validation (CQV), and Quality, Compliance, and Regulatory services, we are looking for individuals who are excited to contribute to impactful projects and drive innovation.

We believe that great things happen when Empathy, Integrity, and Transparency guide our actions. That's why we foster a culture of collaboration, inclusion, and continuous development – empowering our team members to grow personally and professionally.

Position Overview

The Junior Automation Project Engineer supports the delivery of automation systems for pharmaceutical or biotechnology projects under the guidance of senior automation and project engineers. The role focuses on supporting project execution activities, including documentation preparation, testing coordination, vendor interaction, and site-based support to ensure automation systems are delivered in compliance with GMP and overall project requirements.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support automation activities for manufacturing equipment, process skids, and utility-related automation systems, as applicable
  • Assist in coordinating automation tasks across project phases, including design, installation, testing, and handover to operations
  • Track automation-related action items, issues, and deliverables to support timely project execution
  • Assist in the preparation, maintenance, and review of automation documentation, including Functional Specifications (FS), configuration summaries, and FAT/SAT/IQ/OQ protocols and reports
  • Support document formatting, version control, and controlled document management in line with GMP requirements
  • Assist with deviation management and change control documentation under supervision
  • Support FAT and SAT execution with vendors and system integrators
  • Assist with on-site commissioning and testing activities such as loop checks, functional testing, and alarm and interlock verification
  • Record test results, support issue tracking, and assist with closure of punch list items
  • Coordinate with automation vendors, system integrators, project engineering teams, CQV, and QA teams to support site readiness and testing activities
  • Participate in project meetings, workshops, and technical discussions as required
  • Build working knowledge of automation platforms (PLC, SCADA, DCS), the GMP automation lifecycle, and project execution workflows
  • Follow established procedures and best practices while proactively seeking guidance and feedback to develop technical competency

Required Qualifications

  • Diploma or Bachelors degree in Automation Engineering, Electrical/Electronics Engineering, Mechatronics, Chemical Engineering, or a related discipline
  • 2–5 years of experience in automation, engineering, or project support roles
  • Internship or project exposure within pharmaceutical, biotech, or manufacturing environments is an advantage
  • Fresh graduates with relevant academic training or project experience are welcome to apply
  • Basic understanding of PLC, SCADA, and DCS concepts, industrial automation systems, and GMP documentation practices
  • Familiarity with automation drawings such as I/O lists and control diagrams, as well as testing and commissioning concepts
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

Why join us?

  • Generous Leave Policy.
  • Comprehensive Health & Wellness Coverage: Receive robust medical and dental coverage to support your health needs.
  • Professional Development Opportunities: Access continuous learning programs and resources for career growth within the company.
  • Inclusive Culture: Work in an environment where innovation, collaboration, and diverse perspectives are encouraged and celebrated.
  • Shape the Future: Make a real difference in the pharmaceutical industry while advancing your career.

How to apply

Are you ready to play a key role in shaping the future of pharmaceutical solutions? If you're passionate about making an impact, we want you on our team!

Please submit your resume, outlining your qualifications and experience relevant to the role, here.

postedCompany.description:
Incorporated in 2007, No deviation is a patient-centric solution provider for the pharmaceutical industry.

We are experts in engineering consultancy, commissioning, qualification and validation services, and regulatory quality/compliance in Asia and Europe.
postedCompany.name: NO DEVIATION PTE. LTD.
